# Dynatrace AI Workspace

A pre-configured AI assistant workspace for Dynatrace observability workflows, compatible with both Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. Skills, prompts, and MCP server connections are ready to use — load the workspace and start investigating without any additional setup.

## Core Purpose

This workspace teaches AI assistants how to work with a live Dynatrace environment. It combines domain knowledge (agent skills), reusable task templates (prompts), direct platform access via the Dynatrace MCP server, and the dtctl CLI for terminal-level operations — so an AI can answer observability questions, run DQL queries, investigate problems, and verify results end-to-end without additional configuration.

## Key Components

**MCP Servers:**
Two live Dynatrace environments are pre-wired: `production-mcp` (default) and `sprint-mcp` (fallback). Specify which server to use at the start of a session.

**dtctl CLI:**
A kubectl-style CLI for Dynatrace that complements MCP-based investigations with terminal-level access. Used for verification steps — confirming notebooks and other artifacts created by AI workflows actually exist and are correctly structured in Dynatrace.

**Agent Skills:**
13 domain knowledge packages load automatically when relevant. They cover DQL query fundamentals, service monitoring, frontend/RUM analytics, distributed tracing, infrastructure (hosts, Kubernetes, AWS), logging, problem analysis, dashboards, notebooks, migration, and the dtctl CLI.

**Prompts / Slash Commands:**
7 task templates cover common workflows: daily standup, health check, error investigation, problem troubleshooting, incident response, performance regression, and standup with notebook creation (the last one combines MCP-based analysis, Dynatrace notebook creation, and dtctl verification in a single end-to-end workflow).

## Operational Rule

Always start with Dynatrace problems before querying logs or metrics. The built-in prompts enforce this automatically.

## Technical Details

Skills follow a `dt-<domain>[-<usecase>]` naming convention with a `SKILL.md` and optional `references/` subdirectory. Load `dt-dql-essentials` before writing any DQL queries. The `dtctl` skill covers the Dynatrace CLI for settings, configuration, and operational tasks beyond what the MCP server exposes.
